name: Linear API
what_it_does: GraphQL API for Linear's issue tracking and project management platform, enabling agents to create, query, update, and triage issues, cycles, and projects programmatically.
use_cases: Automatically create and assign Linear issues from CI/CD pipeline failures, monitoring alerts, or code review findings
Query open issues assigned to a team or user to build prioritized work queues for engineering agents
Update issue status, priority, and assignee as an agent completes work steps in an automated workflow
Subscribe to Linear webhooks to trigger downstream agent actions when issues are created, updated, or transitioned
Generate sprint summaries and roadmap status reports by querying cycles, projects, and issue completion rates
not_for: Non-engineering teams — Linear is designed for software development workflows and lacks features like CRM, HR, or general business process management
Agents needing a no-code or rich document/wiki workspace alongside issue tracking
Organizations already standardized on Jira or Asana where migrating tooling would create more friction than value
best_when: An engineering agent needs fast, reliable programmatic access to a modern issue tracker with a clean GraphQL schema and strong webhook support.
avoid_when: The team uses a different issue tracker or the use case requires rich document management, time tracking, or non-engineering project workflows not supported by Linear.

auth.notes: Supports both personal API keys (full access to the authenticated user's organizations) and OAuth 2.0 for third-party integrations with granular scope control. OAuth scopes include read and write permissions for issues, comments, teams, and more. API keys are preferred for agent-to-agent automation; OAuth is recommended for user-facing integrations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan allows up to 250 issues; API access is included on all plans.
pricing.paid_tiers: Standard at $8/user/month; Plus at $14/user/month; Enterprise pricing on request with advanced security and compliance features.
pricing.notes: API access is not rate-limited differently by plan tier; all plans share the same API rate limits. Free plan issue limit makes it unsuitable for production agent workflows at scale.

performance.rate_limits: 1,500 requests per hour per API key; rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset) are included in every response.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL errors are returned in the standard errors array with extensions.userPresentableMessage and extensions.code. Error codes are consistent and documented, making them reliable for programmatic branching in agent logic.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Linear mutations are not idempotent by default. Agents creating issues should check for existing issues by identifier or external ID before creating to avoid duplicates. No native idempotency key support.
known_agent_gotchas: The GraphQL schema uses connection types with edges/nodes pagination; agents expecting simple array responses must traverse the connection structure or risk missing paginated results.
Rate limit of 1,500 req/hour is shared across all operations including introspection; agents that introspect the schema on every request will exhaust limits quickly.
Issue identifiers (e.g., ENG-123) are team-scoped and human-readable but not stable across team renames; agents should use the UUID id field for stable references.
Webhook delivery does not include retry guarantees; agents must implement their own deduplication and idempotency when processing Linear webhook events.
The Node.js SDK is the only officially maintained SDK; agents using other languages must construct raw GraphQL queries, and community SDKs may lag behind schema changes.

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth 2.0 with granular scopes is a strong model. Personal API keys have full access and should be stored in secrets managers. SOC2 Type II certified. EU data residency available.
